satire_attempt: Welcome to the cinematic universe of your codebase, in which you are merely a temporary resident of the Marvel-style middle act. Phase one was the prequel ticket nobody read. Phase three will be the residual streaming series in some future engineer's head. Buy my book Three-Act Code: How To Stop Caring About Your Production Software For Just $19.99.
verdict_reason: The satire requires the verse to advocate detachment from the middle phase, but the verse advocates correct sizing — the middle phase is real and operational, just not the whole of the lifecycle. The notifications-service example resists satire because it concretely names what each phase contains. The verse's claim survives the satire.
